project.name: SylphxAI Benchmark
project.summary: Benchmark hub for JavaScript and TypeScript library performance research, result snapshots, scoring methodology, and supporting benchmark automation.
goals: Maintain reproducible benchmark categories for JavaScript and TypeScript libraries.
Store benchmark result snapshots, methodology notes, and generated ranking documentation.
Provide CLI and workflow surfaces for running, rebuilding, and publishing benchmark evidence.
nonGoals: Own the libraries being benchmarked or their release decisions.
Own product runtime behavior in repositories that consume benchmark findings.
Own enterprise engineering doctrine, standards, or project manifest schema.

owns.name: benchmark-framework
owns.description: CLI, core scoring, result generation, and category scaffolding code for benchmark execution.

owns.name: benchmark-evidence
owns.description: Committed benchmark result snapshots, generated READMEs, methodology notes, and comparison reports.

doesNotOwn: The upstream libraries, frameworks, or package versions being measured.
Product dependency decisions or runtime changes in repositories that consume benchmark findings.
Enterprise doctrine and manifest schema owned by SylphxAI/doctrine.

allowedDependencies.surface: project manifest schema and engineering doctrine

forbiddenCouplings: Do not encode product-specific runtime choices as benchmark framework behavior.
Do not treat benchmark ranking docs as authority for product changes without a product-owned PR or ADR.
Do not fork doctrine standards into this repository.

delivery.deployPath: not-applicable: benchmark evidence is committed to Git and served through GitHub; workflows update or rebuild result artifacts.
delivery.productionProof: Successful benchmark workflow or local CLI run plus committed result/readme snapshots and doctrine project-control audit.

gaps.description: Benchmark workflows exist but no required PR admission or postsubmit proof context protects benchmark framework changes.
gaps.target: central ADR-29 required-context rollout or repo-local benchmark validation gate

gaps.description: Benchmark result READMEs and ranking docs are generated/current-state surfaces; add freshness checks before relying on them as no-human governance evidence.
gaps.target: next benchmark framework or scoring-methodology change
